title: refreshable materialized view, don't full cleanup old destination table after new destination table with new UUID
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
After create refreshable materialized view, it recreate destination table with new uuid on every refresh, it's OK, but on file system refresh process don't cleanup old table properly, and I can't create destination table with the same UUID
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
use following commands for reproduce
```sql
DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS test SYNC;
CREATE DATABASE test ENGINE=Atomic;
CREATE TABLE  IF NOT EXISTS `test`.`mv_src_table` UUID 'dc60928b-ff47-448d-806c-2dc2e06714fb' ON CLUSTER 'cluster' (id UInt64) Engine=ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/{database}/{table}','replica1') ORDER BY id;
CREATE  TABLE  IF NOT EXISTS `test`.`mv_refreshable_dst_table` UUID '620be150-bdef-47b2-a295-428dd8e79c97' ON CLUSTER 'cluster' (id UInt64) Engine=MergeTree ORDER BY id;
CREATE  MATERIALIZED VIEW  IF NOT EXISTS `test`.`mv_refreshable` UUID '98be4ca8-fe55-471a-9c56-2e722a7cd8c1' ON CLUSTER 'cluster' REFRESH EVERY 1 MINUTE TO `test`.`mv_refreshable_dst_table` AS SELECT max(id) AS id FROM `test`.`mv_src_table`;
DROP TABLE `test`.`mv_refreshable_dst_table` SYNC;
-- INEXPECTED ERROR HERE
CREATE  TABLE  IF NOT EXISTS `test`.`mv_refreshable_dst_table` UUID '620be150-bdef-47b2-a295-428dd8e79c97' ON CLUSTER 'cluster' (id UInt64) Engine=MergeTree ORDER BY id;
```
root reason
After creating MATERIALIZED VIEW, I tried to check the old UUID and got a non-empty file structure, but the expected result was that the folder should not exist.
```bash
ls -la /var/lib/clickhouse/store/620/620be150-bdef-47b2-a295-428dd8e79c97/
total 16
drwxr-x--- 3 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 05:32 .
drwxr-x--- 3 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 05:32 ..
drwxr-x--- 2 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 05:32 detached
-rw-r----- 1 clickhouse clickhouse    1 Sep 10 05:32 format_version.txt
```
and `mv_refreshable_dst_table` contains different uuid as expected
```sql
SELECT uuid FROM system.tables WHERE database='test' AND table='mv_refreshable_dst_table';
┌─uuid─────────────────────────────────┐
1. │ 38ca3e2a-4f8c-4d54-99c9-6b4b00c94968 │
```
and data present
```
root@clickhouse:/# ls -la /var/lib/clickhouse/store/38c/38ca3e2a-4f8c-4d54-99c9-6b4b00c94968/
total 20
drwxr-x--- 4 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 06:00 .
drwxr-x--- 3 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 06:00 ..
drwxr-x--- 2 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 06:00 all_1_1_0
drwxr-x--- 2 clickhouse clickhouse 4096 Sep 10 06:00 detached
-rw-r----- 1 clickhouse clickhouse    1 Sep 10 06:00 format_version.txt
```
also reproduced on 25.3
i think this is just present from the beginning: REFRESHABLE MATERIALIZED VIEW just create junk empty folders in store for every refresh
### Expected behavior
properly cleanup store sub-folders after refresh materialized view
### Error message and/or stacktrace
got error
bug_features_seed: DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS test CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test.mv_src_table (id BIGINT, name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test.mv_src_table (id, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'test1', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'test2', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test.mv_refreshable_dst_table (id BIGINT, value DOUBLE PRECISION);
CREATE VIEW test.mv_refreshable AS SELECT id, MAX(id) * 1.0 AS value FROM test.mv_src_table GROUP BY id;
key_bug_features: incomplete cleanup of old destination table on refresh
